page: Amazon Web Services r8i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores from an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at 3.9 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 480 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for tile and int8 operations. The instance is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier capabilities in multi-core processing and memory throughput. The multi-core stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests place this instance in the top 10% of benchmarked servers, while single-core performance remains in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests show exceptional read and write speeds, with cached read-only small block bandwidth reaching 10218.84 GB/sec and uncached large block read bandwidth at 383.47 GB/sec. Additionally, the instance excels in database operations and Redis SET operations, achieving 218.29 million operations per second.

The r8i.32xlarge is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, and high-throughput web serving. It also demonstrates strong performance in CPU-based machine learning tasks, with top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds for medium and large language models. The primary tradeoff is the lack of local NVMe storage, requiring external network-attached storage for persistent data. The high density of DDR5 memory and dedicated compute resources offer qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise-scale in-memory databases and data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 1024.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. While the instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, it provides 50 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database operation speeds, though single-core performance sits in the middle tier. This hardware profile is highly efficient for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, including in-memory databases, Redis deployments, and large-scale data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.32xlarge features 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring DDR5 RAM and advanced Intel Xeon processors for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 1024 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with AMX and AVX-512 support
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis, and large-scale data processing
